channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Former President Donald Trump to make stop in Austin on Friday
item.description: As the 2024 U.S. presidential race heads into the final stretch, the Republican nominee announced Wednesday that he will be in Texas on the same day this week as his Democratic challenger.
item.dc:creator: Xiomara Moore, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump says China's leader will bully Harris 'like a baby' as allies try to infantilize her
item.description: Donald Trump has said China’s leader would handle Vice President Kamala Harris “like a baby” if she’s elected to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Dan Merica, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Iranian armed forces plan to triple Revolutionary Guard budget thanks to oil exports
item.description: Iran's military forces will get a large influx of cash thanks to a booming energy economy, despite Western sanctions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Like Biden, Harris puts focus on Trump as Election Day draws near
item.description: When Joe Biden was running for reelection, his strategy was to keep the focus squarely on Donald Trump and his treat to democratic institutions.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: In rare move, Pentagon denies falsehood about troops allowed to use force during election
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In a rare move, the Pentagon strongly pushed back Thursday against misinformation spread on social media that falsely suggests U.S. troops have been authorized to use force against American citizens during the election.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ship owner in Baltimore bridge collapse agrees to pay $102 million for cleanup
item.description: The owner and manager of the cargo ship that caused the Baltimore bridge collapse have agreed to pay more than $100 million to settle a lawsuit brought by the Justice Department.
item.dc:creator: Lea Skene And Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Montana Senate race shatters spending records at $309 per registered voter
item.description: The Montana Senate race, pitting Sen. Jon Tester against Navy SEAL Tim Sheehy, has broken the record for spending with $309 spent per registered voter.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jamie Dimon walks political tightrope, may be considering Harris cabinet position: report
item.description: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon reportedly said he was considering a position in a potential Harris administration, but sources close to the banking magnate argued that was not the case.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden to apologize for policy sending Native American children to forced-assimilation boarding schools
item.description: President Biden plans to issue an official apology for a U.S. policy that for 150 years sent Native American children to boarding schools where they were stripped of their culture and language.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris is leaning on big names like Obama, Beyoncé and Springsteen to close out her campaign
item.description: Bruce Springsteen and Barack Obama lent their star power to Kamala Harris’ quest for the presidency on Thursday as the vice president enlists some of her most high-profile surrogates in the closing days of the campaign.
item.dc:creator: Dan Merica, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Vast majority of Americans support photo ID requirement to vote, new poll says
item.description: A Gallup Poll released on Thursday shows that most Americans support having photo identification as a requirement to vote in elections.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Beyoncé to campaign with Harris: Sources
item.description: The Grammy-winning artist previously endorsed Harris at the conclusion of the Democratic National Convention in Chicago.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: Kentucky lawmaker dies weeks after lawnmower plunges into swimming pool
item.description: Kentucky state Sen. Johnnie Turner (R) died Tuesday from injuries he suffered after being thrown into an empty swimming pool while riding his lawnmower just over a month ago.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Beyoncé expected to join Harris at Houston rally: Reports
item.description: Beyoncé is poised to get in "Formation" behind Vice President Kamala Harris' White House bid. The music superstar will reportedly be joining the vice president at a Friday campaign rally in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fischer's Senate fight in Nebraska leaves Republicans frustrated
item.description: Republicans are upset that the race to protect Sen. Deb Fischer (R-Neb.) in deep red Nebraska has gotten competitive, forcing the party to spend precious resources that could be used elsewhere in its fight for the Senate majority.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: DNC hits Trump over reported praise of Hitler's generals in mobile billboard
item.description: The Democratic National Committee (DNC) is deploying a mobile billboard ahead of former President Donald Trump’s rally in Las Vegas on Thursday, hitting him over his past reported praise of Adolf Hitler’s generals.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Calling out of work for your pet? City Council introduces pet sick day bill
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Sick days may not just be for humans living in New York City in the future. City Councilmembers Shaun Abreu and Tiffany Cabán introduced a bill on Wednesday to let workers take sick days if their pets are ill.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Quarter of young Black men say they will vote for Trump: Survey
item.description: Despite one-fourth of young Black men saying they would cast their ballot for Trump, they repeatedly identify Harris as the strongest candidate on the issues.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, NewsNation

item.title: GOP maps out agenda for Trump’s first 100 days
item.description: Senate and House Republicans are setting the table for what they hope — and increasingly expect — will be unified GOP control of the White House and Congress after the election.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Election trends resurface as Republicans, young voters lead in early participation
item.description: Tampa Bay-area election officials said voting patterns may be going back to past elections -- one-third voting by mail, one-third voting early, and one-third voting on election day.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Assemblywoman Shea Backus discusses education funding, affordable housing in re-election bid
item.description: Assemblywoman Shea Backus, a Democrat running for re-election in District 37, appeared on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide to outline her legislative priorities, focusing on education funding and affordable housing.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: PolitiFact weighs in on false claims about Walz and how to spot disinformation
item.description: A senior U.S. intelligence official has found that Russia is behind viral disinformation about Gov. Tim Walz. An account on X spread false claims against the Democratic vice presidential nominee but a closer look at the posts reveals inconsistencies and inaccuracies.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crann and Ngoc Bui, MPR News

item.title: Harris pitches herself as 'pragmatic capitalist' in push for Latino voters
item.description: Vice President Harris is pitching herself as a “pragmatic capitalist” in a Noticias Telemundo interview as she looks to court Latino voters less than two weeks out from the election. “I am a capitalist.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Axelrod: Harris in a 'terrible place' on Middle East conflict
item.description: Veteran Democratic strategist David Axelrod says Vice President Harris is caught in a “terrible place” on the growing conflict in the Middle East, which threatens to turn off Lebanese and Palestinian American voters in Michigan, a crucial battleground state.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Carville lists 3 reasons why he thinks Harris will win
item.description: “The biggest reason Mr. Trump will lose is that the whole Republican Party has been on a losing streak since Mr. Trump took it over,” he said in an essay published Wednesday by The New York Times.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Efforts by Russia, Iran and China to sway U.S. voters may escalate, new Microsoft report says
item.description: A new threat intelligence report from Microsoft says foreign adversaries have showed continued determination to influence the U.S. election and there are signs their activity will intensify as Election Day nears.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: USPS urges voters not to delay mailing their ballots
item.description: Top officials at the U.S. Postal Service are urging voters to mail in their ballots at least one week before Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Raquel Martin, Fox 5

item.title: Blinken urges Israel to seek enduring deal, as officials say a limited cease-fire has been discussed
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken says Israel needs to pursue an “enduring strategic success” in Gaza after its tactical victories against Hamas. He urged it Wednesday to seek a deal that would end the war and bring back dozens of hostages.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Immigration, oil and gas jobs hot issues in volatile House race
item.description: Border Report is featuring stories on closely contested House of Representatives races in border districts that could determine which party controls the lower chamber of Congress for the next two years.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Jailed reporters, silenced networks: What Trump says he'd do to the media if elected
item.description: On the campaign trail, former President Donald Trump has threatened to jail reporters who won't give up the sources of leaks and to strip networks of their broadcast licenses for fact-checking him.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: These are the 2 paths either Trump or Harris has to follow to become president
item.description: With seven states up for grabs, here are the likely scenarios that would put either candidate in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Russia is behind viral disinformation targeting Walz, intelligence official says
item.description: Groups in Russia created and helped spread viral disinformation targeting Democratic vice presidential candidate Tim Walz, a senior U.S. intelligence official said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Western Wisconsin race has strong implications for congressional control
item.description: Wisconsin’s third district makes up most of western Wisconsin. On the ballot this year, voters will choose between incumbent Republican Congressman Derrick Van Orden and Democrat Rebecca Cooke. The region has historically been a swing district the elected Barack Obama and later Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Aleesa Kuznetsov, MPR News

item.title: Early voting kicks off in battleground Wisconsin with a push from Obama and Walz
item.description: In-person early voting has kicked off across battleground Wisconsin, with former President Barack Obama and Democratic vice presidential nominee Tim Walz hosting a rally in liberal Madison.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Harris bets her policies can attract Latino voters while Trump touts his time as president to them
item.description: Both Kamala Harris and Donald Trump are highlighting their economic policies in their outreach to Latino voters with Election Day just two weeks away.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Central Park Five sue Trump for defamation over debate comments
item.description: The exonerated Central Park Five sued former President Donald Trump for defamation on Monday over his comments at the recent presidential debate about their wrongful convictions for rape and assault.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Disaster unemployment assistance available after Hurricane Milton
item.description: The Florida Commerce announced that Disaster Unemployment Assistance (DUA), is now available to businesses and residents whose employment or self-employment was lost or interrupted by Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
